channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘El diablo viste a la moda’ ha evolucionado con su audiencia
item.description: Los que llegamos a la madurez junto con la protagonista ahora podemos ver que amar tu trabajo no tiene por qué significar convertirte en Miranda Priestly. Puede significar ser Andy Sachs.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: Soy infelizmente soltera. ¿Tengo que asistir a la boda de mi amiga?
item.description: Nuestra columnista de Pregúntale a la terapeuta, Lori Gottlieb, aconseja a una lectora que no quiere que le recuerden su soledad una vez más.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb

item.title: Por qué las parejas ya no quieren tener bebés
item.description: ¿Es el dinero que ya no alcanza o es el pánico por lo que pueda venir?
item.dc:creator: Anna Louie Sussman

media:content.media:credit: Em Wall para The New York Times

item.title: New York’s Budget Deal Is Still Hazy. Here Are 5 Key Questions.
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul claimed victory for a state budget agreement that is already five weeks overdue, and clearly still needs more work.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul, who is facing re-election this year, announced the framework of a $268 billion state budget on Thursday, but acknowledged that some issues still needed to be resolved.

item.title: Picture Prompt: Top Secret
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: Gates Around Washington Square Park? Greenwich Village Is Divided.
item.description: The park in Manhattan, known for its regal arch and freewheeling spirit, has a hard-to-enforce midnight curfew.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:description: Washington Square Park in Manhattan is supposed to be off limits from midnight to 6 a.m., but people often ignore this curfew.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 8, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Recaps Rubio’s Peacemaking Visit to Pope Leo
item.description: Jimmy Fallon joked that President Trump and Pope Leo are putting Marco Rubio, who is Catholic, “in the middle and seeing which parent he goes to.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa mitigate
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 350 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Philip Caputo, Who Wrote Blistering Vietnam War Memoir, Dies at 84
item.description: “A Rumor of War,” about his service as a Marine Corps infantry officer and published in 1977, relentlessly detailed “the things men do in war and the things war does to them.”
item.dc:creator: Joseph Berger

media:content.media:credit: Frank Lennon/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Philip Caputo in Canada, in 1987.

item.title: Algunos objetos del Titanic podrían subastarse pronto
item.description: La última vez que se propuso una venta, la idea suscitó objeciones de los gobiernos estadounidense y francés, así como de la UNESCO y otras instituciones culturales.
item.dc:creator: Marc Davis

item.title: Word of the Day: mitigate
item.description: This word has appeared in 350 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S. and Iran Test Truce With Military Strikes
item.description: Oil prices rose after the United States attacked Iranian military sites in retaliation for Iran firing on U.S. warships in the Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: El nuevo gobierno de Hungría examina la relación de Orbán con China
item.description: Pekín dependía del líder saliente de Hungría, Viktor Orbán, para afianzarse en Europa. Una gigantesca fábrica de baterías resultó ser un paso demasiado audaz.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai

media:content.media:credit: Akos Stiller para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La fábrica de baterías Contemporary Amperex Technology Ltd, o CATL, en Debrecen, Hungría, la semana pasada. La oposición a la planta hizo que los votantes locales se mostraran descontentos con el primer ministro Viktor Orbán en las elecciones de abril.

item.title: Live Updates: Early U.K. Election Results Point to Big Losses for Starmer’s Party
item.description: Votes are still being tallied in thousands of local races across Britain, but hundreds of candidates from Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party have already lost their council seats.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: A temporary polling station set up in Battersea, southwest London on Thursday.

item.title: ¿La IA de Google puede planear tus viajes? Nuestro experto la puso a prueba
item.description: Gemini es una navaja suiza digital para planificar vuelos, actividades e itinerarios, pero no es perfecta. ¿Por qué se le olvidó incluir la ropa interior en la lista de equipaje?
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen

item.title: Is Trump About to Invite In the Biggest Predator in the World?
item.description: Chinese automakers do not belong on American soil.
item.dc:creator: Oren Cass

item.title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
item.description: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Berry Wang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Center for Strategic and International Studies
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in Busan, South Korea, in October. Both sides agreed to extend a trade truce to limit tariffs.

item.title: What Virus Experts Want You to Know About Hantavirus
item.description: News of a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has raised concerns about what this means for the broader public. Nina Agrawal, a reporter for Well, speaks with Dr. Gaby Frank, director of the Special Pathogens Center at Johns Hopkins, to learn more.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Theodore Tae

item.title: Many Russians in No Mood for Celebration on Kremlin’s Biggest Day of the Year
item.description: As the country heads into Victory Day, the economy is stalling, internet restrictions are growing and the Kremlin is fearing Ukrainian attacks.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Nanna Heitmann
item.category: Communist Party of the Russian Federation

media:content.media:description: Red Square in Moscow. The Victory Day procession, one of the year’s biggest events, is staged in the heart of Kremlin power.

item.title: En Los Ángeles, la alcaldesa se enfrenta a rivales de los extremos políticos
item.description: Nithya Raman, concejala liberal, y Spencer Pratt, estrella republicana de la telerrealidad, son los principales contrincantes de Karen Bass, quien busca su segundo mandato.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler, Jill Cowan and Conor Dougherty

media:content.media:description: La alcaldesa Karen Bass nunca se recuperó del todo de no haber estado en California cuando Pacific Palisades estalló en llamas el año pasado.

item.title: Corrections: May 8, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, May 8, 2026.

item.title: Writing About My Mother as if She Were Dead
item.description: I needed distance to write honestly about my childhood. But fiction didn’t protect any of us from pain.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Landis

item.title: They Met One Night in Queens. A Year in St. Lucia Followed.
item.description: A chance meeting in Queens led Shanae Strachan and Lance Growth to build a life between New York and St. Lucia before marrying in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: Unmistakable Love of Austin, the Texas Longhorns and Each Other
item.description: Adam Mena and Jesse Stowell brought Austin, Texas, alive at their wedding with a taco truck, burnt orange hues and two miniature shaggy Highland cows.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: She Had Seen Her in Photos. Then They Met in Real Life.
item.description: Nandika Chatterjee had heard about Abigail Braggins from a roommate and thought she was “cute.” A wave in Little Italy sealed their fate.
item.dc:creator: Stella Raine Chu

item.title: After a Secret Was Revealed, Their First Wedding Was Canceled
item.description: “Everybody deserves a second chance,” said Negarin Sadr of Matt Sornson ahead of their “Persian winter wonderland” wedding in Switzerland. (“But not a third chance.”)
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
item.description: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: David Payr for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A worker in Vienna installing a heat pump for the company Heizma, which said it had record sales in March and April.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Retirement, Then Back To the Grind
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, May 8, 2026.

item.title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
item.description: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas

media:content.media:credit: Alisha Jucevic for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The State Department is reviewing the 53 Mexican consulates in the U.S., including the consulate in Los Angeles.

item.title: Trump Says Cease-Fire Intact After Trade of Attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president dismissed Iran’s firing on U.S. vessels in the strait as a mere “trifle.”
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump visiting the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool on the National Mall in Washington on Thursday.

item.title: This Pulitzer-Winning Novel Traps You in One Sentence Forever
item.description: The Pulitzer winner Daniel Kraus says his novel “Angel Down,” set in France during World War I, is designed to trap readers inside it forever. On a recent episode of the “Book Review” podcast, Kraus explains why he wrote the book as one continuous sentence and how the structure reflects the endless cycle of violence at the center of the story. Tap the link in our bio to hear the full conversation.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 08, 2026
item.description: Rafael Musa and Geoffrey Schorkopf are back with another stellar themeless.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: This challenging Friday puzzle isn’t so easily cracked.

item.title: Jason Meade Found Guilty in Fatal Shooting of Casey Goodson Jr. in Ohio
item.description: Jason Meade had been searching for a fugitive when he shot Casey Goodson Jr. in 2020. It is rare for a law enforcement official to be convicted in a killing that occurs on the job.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:description: Jason Meade in court in Columbus, Ohio, on Wednesday.

item.title: State Department Will Revoke Passports of Parents Who Owe Child Support
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to more strictly enforce a 1996 law that gives the State Department the authority to revoke passports over unpaid child support.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:description: It is unclear how many citizens could have their passports revoked under the policy.

item.title: Canvas Online Learning Platform Disabled After Breach by Hackers
item.description: Most users regained access to the platform hours after a hacking group said it had attacked Canvas’s parent company and breached 275 million people’s data.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Harvard is one of the schools that experienced Canvas outages on Thursday.

item.title: Cornell Is Investigating Confrontation Between President and Students
item.description: The university’s leader bumped into students as he drove away following an evening of debate over the long-running conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Heather Ainsworth for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cornell University students say the university president hit them with his vehicle. He says he was the victim in the incident.

item.title: Alaska Can Resume Killing Bears to Protect Caribou Herd, Judge Says
item.description: The state program employs helicopters and small planes to shoot black and brown bears of any age in a large area of southwestern Alaska. Two conservation groups have sued to stop it.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:credit: Acacia Johnson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brown bears in Alaska. The state culls bears in the spring, during the caribou calving season. Nearly 200 bears were killed between 2023 and 2025, according to a lawsuit from two conservation groups.

item.title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Eric Schmitt and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia at the White House with President Trump in November.

item.title: U.S. and Zambia Feud: Trump Health Aid Deal Stalls Over Critical Minerals
item.description: The tussle put a spotlight on the administration’s attempt to replace the United States Agency for International Development with a new, “America First” alternative.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Rabecca Lungu
item.category: United States Agency for International Development

media:content.media:description: A man working with silicon manganese at the Chinese-owned Datong Processing plant in Kabwe, Zambia, in 2024. Some analysts say that the Trump administration has used foreign aid to try to pressure Zambia to provide greater mining access.

item.title: DOGE’s Termination of Humanities Grants Is Ruled Unconstitutional
item.description: A lawsuit against the National Endowment for the Humanities drew wide attention for revealing how DOGE had used ChatGPT to cancel grants.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler

media:content.media:description: The National Endowment for the Humanities, created in 1965, supports scholarship, historical sites and public projects across the country.

item.title: Fetterman Says He Has ‘No Plans’ to Leave the Democratic Party
item.description: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Senator John Fetterman, Democrat of Pennsylvania, wrote in an opinion piece in The Washington Post this week that the Democratic Party had veered off course in its stance on immigration and the war in Iran.

item.title: Here’s How Freaked Out You Should Be About the Hantavirus Cruise Ship
item.description: An expert weighs in.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Rivers

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iranian Targets; Iran Says It Returned Fire
item.description: The exchange was the latest twist in a week of mixed signals in the region and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump searches for an off-ramp in the war that he started.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman, on Wednesday.

item.title: ‘Remarkably Bright Creatures’ Review: Their Octopus Teacher
item.description: Sally Field and Lewis Pullman get help from an eight-tentacled friend in this bland adaptation of a hit novel.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: Louisiana Asks Supreme Court to Halt Abortion Pill Access by Mail
item.description: The request comes after a federal appeals court temporarily blocked a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mifepristone is typically delivered as part of a two-drug regimen through the first 12 weeks of pregnancy.

item.title: A Stroll Through the Mansions of New York’s Gilded Age
item.description: Yes, some memorable mansions were lost to the vagaries of expenses and development, but many remain — and some are even open to the public. A walking guide.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyall

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Andrew Carnegie residence on 91st Street is now the Cooper Hewitt, Smithsonian Design Museum.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes as Tensions in Strait of Hormuz Rise
item.description: The escalating attacks raise the threat that the cease-fire could break down.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: This photo released by U.S. Central Command Public Affairs shows the aircraft carrier U.S.S. George H.W. Bush on Sunday.

item.title: Dealers Face a Choice: Show Solo Artists? Or Groups?
item.description: The decision can vary from fair to fair but sometimes solo booths can help visitors focus their attention on a single artist, rather than being overwhelmed by the art of many.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Keenan
